note: Wallet & Backup Security canonical doc rewritten and expanded with five new sections covering everything that surfaced during the encrypted-backup walkthrough on the laptop wallet. (1) Section 1 reworked: the previous wording implied the CLI active wallet was AES-256-GCM encrypted; it is not — it is plain JSON. The new 'Three storage layers, three encryption models' table makes the distinction unambiguous: CLI active wallet = plain JSON (never cloud), CLI .enc backup = scrypt + AES-256-GCM (cloud-safe), web wallet localStorage = PBKDF2 + AES-256-GCM. Why the active CLI wallet is plaintext is explained: the miner cannot prompt for a password every block. (2) Section 3 Layer 2 split into CLI vs web wallet paths — the CLI path now points at wallet-export --encrypted as the cloud-safe export instead of suggesting a raw cp of wallet.json to a remote host. (3) Section 7 cloud Q&A rewritten to call out the file-type distinction explicitly: CLI active wallet on Drive = unsafe, .enc on Drive = safe. (4) Section 9 NEW: encrypted backup commands cheat sheet (wallet-export, wallet-import, dumpprivkey) plus a 3-secret table (12 words / .enc passphrase / web wallet password) so users do not conflate them. (5) Section 10 NEW: the CLI mining label is freeform — verified at src/sost-cli.cpp:1269 and src/wallet.cpp:84 — with two worked examples (phase2-miner / home-rig). (6) Section 11 NEW: the HD vs getnewaddress trap — hd create gives an address recoverable from the 12 words; getnewaddress gives a random key NOT recoverable from any seed. Recommendation: mine to hd-seed directly, not to a getnewaddress label. (7) Section 12 NEW: local cache vs chain truth — explains why wallet-import shows a small Balance: figure while listaddresses (chain truth) shows the real on-chain total. Reassures that send always resyncs from the node before signing, so spendable balance is always the chain truth. (8) Section 13 NEW: TX and block limits cheat sheet (16 KB policy, 100 KB consensus, 1 MB block, 4096 tx default, 65536 hard cap, 600 s spacing) plus a throughput table by transaction shape. Both files English-only, mirrored byte-for-byte in markdown and HTML. NO consensus, NO RPC schema, NO node, NO miner, NO transaction wire format. Documentation only.
